headline: A Red Alert Over El Obeid
deck: The UN's rights chief issued a rare public red alert as the RSF massed around North Kordofan's capital, drones hit schools and displacement sites, and cholera took hold under the siege. Half a million remain inside the ring, and the EU and UN's aid chief have both stepped in.

art.title: The Gate: Darfur to Central Sudan
art.caption: El Obeid, capital of North Kordofan, sits on the route between RSF-held Darfur and the army-held centre; the UN has issued a red alert over the massing siege · Map: Sprockett, Escalation Desk · Terrain: NOAA ETOPO1

body: Volker Türk, the United Nations' top human-rights official, raised a public red alert over El Obeid, warning of a "catastrophe" as the Rapid Support Forces massed troops around the North Kordofan capital and drones hit schools, a site sheltering displaced people, and fuel stations, with about half a million civilians still inside the ring, roughly a hundred thousand of them already displaced [E1]. UNICEF says attacks in North Kordofan have killed and wounded children through the spring, part of a toll of at least 330 children killed or injured across Sudan in the first half of the year [E2]. A cholera outbreak has now taken hold under the siege.

The alarm is no longer only in Geneva. The European Union issued a high-level statement on the escalating violence in El Obeid, and the UN's humanitarian chief spoke directly with the RSF's commander to press for safe civilian access [E3][E1]. Last week the Human Rights Council's fact-finding mission had already found that the RSF's assault on El Fasher bore the markers of genocide and opened an urgent inquiry into El Obeid, warning the same tactics were turning on it [E1]. A red alert is the register officials reach for when they believe the next atrocity is being staged, not merely feared.

The city's value is geographic. El Obeid straddles the route between the RSF-controlled Darfur region and the army-held centre and east, and Al Jazeera describes its fall as the point at which Sudan's war would enter a new phase, opening the road into the country's middle [E4]. That is why the paramilitary has spent months encircling it rather than going around [E4].

A massing force is not yet an assault. The record shows encirclement, a drone campaign, a spreading epidemic and a rights chief sounding an alarm; it does not show that a ground offensive has begun or that any single outcome is locked [E1][E4]. The RSF still carries an incentive to avoid a second documented genocide finding, and sieges have lifted before [E1].

The discriminators are the same grim few. Whether aid corridors open, whether the drones stop, or whether the ground clashes the UN anticipates arrive, will decide if the red alert reads later as a warning heeded or a warning filed. For the half million inside El Obeid, the alert is the difference the day turns on [E1][E2].

evidence_box.fragment: Volker Türk red alert over El Obeid: "catastrophe"; RSF massing, drone strikes on schools and displacement sites; ~500,000 at risk, ~100,000 displaced; fact-finding mission's El Fasher genocide markers and urgent El Obeid inquiry
At least 330 children killed or injured in Sudan during the first six months of 2026; child casualties in North Kordofan since May
EU high-representative statement on the escalating violence in El Obeid, Sudan
Why El Obeid matters as Sudan's war enters a new phase: the city sits on the route between RSF-held Darfur and the army-held centre and east
